player safety through session recovery without forced repetition

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: level progression toward final confrontation with Joker and Harley Quinn

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: themed environments transition from urban theater → frozen building → graveyard → amusement park

environmental_storytelling.example: theater stage for Mad Hatter encounter, graveyard for Scarecrow, amusement park for Joker/Harley confrontation
frozen office building visually communicates Mr. Freeze's active threat and control over Gotham infrastructure
four-level structure (Game Gear) moves from specific villain encounters toward climactic confrontation with primary antagonist

environmental_storytelling.technique: art deco visual style (SNES version noted)
environmental_storytelling.example: maintains Batman: The Animated Series aesthetic, communicating franchise continuity and world identity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: unlimited batarang projectile attacks; player-controlled movement and attack selection
automatic switch to melee attacks dealing increased damage
appear as stage-end encounters; require specific defeat mechanics matching villain identity
distributed through levels as combatants; defeated through projectile or melee attacks
instant respawn at death location with full health bar and restored ammunition

notes: Genesis version emphasizes run-and-gun action with co-op Batman/Robin teamup mechanics. Villain encounters directly reflect Mr. Freeze's plot to liberate Gotham rogues gallery. Environmental theming (theater, frozen building, graveyard, amusement park) serves dual function: visual storytelling of villain identity + level design variation. Limited source detail on exact visual asset descriptions; analysis derived from gameplay mechanics described and villain thematic associations.
